facts.text: Founded in 1869 by Alexander Turney Stewart, Garden City was one of America’s first planned villages, featuring wide avenues, a 30‑acre park, and a grand hotel.
Cornelia Stewart erected the Cathedral of the Incarnation, a Gothic Revival landmark that also houses the Bishop’s Residence, honoring her late husband.
Stewart’s Central Railroad of Long Island, line he built, first linked Garden City to Long Island City, turning the village into a transport hub.
Long Island’s oldest aviation museum, the Cradle of Aviation, sits in Garden City, celebrating the area’s deep flight history.
The historic Garden City Hotel once hosted Charles Lindbergh’s press corps before his flight, though he never actually stayed there.
Founded in 1947 as part of Adelphi University, the Waldorf School of Garden City was among the first Waldorf schools in the United States.
